voicePractice.readScript: Read the script aloud and clearly to receive instant feedback on your performance.
voicePractice.pastAttemptsDesc: Review your previous attempts and track your vocal improvement over time.
voicePractice.clarityDesc: How accurately you pronounced the words compared to the script. Higher score means clearer delivery.
voicePractice.energyDesc: Pitch variation in your voice while reading. Higher score means richer, less monotone delivery.
voicePractice.paceDesc: The rate of speech measured in words per minute. Ideal MC pace is typically 130-150 WPM.
voicePractice.recordToUnlock: Record your practice session to unlock detailed technical reports and coaching insights.
voicePractice.aiListening: AI is listening to your voice...
voicePractice.overallScoreDesc: Your overall score is calculated based on Clarity (45%), Energy (35%), and Pace (20%).
voicePractice.coverageDesc: Percentage of the script you successfully delivered compared to the total words.
voicePractice.paceSummaryDesc: Your average speaking rate. Professional MCs adjust pace based on the venue and content.
voicePractice.micAccessDenied: Microphone access denied. Please check your settings.
voicePractice.mentorDesc: Get personalized feedback from professional MC mentors.
voicePractice.sampleComparisonNote: Compares the shape of your intonation (pitch rise/fall) — not used for scoring, for visual reference only.
voicePractice.pitchContourWaiting: Waiting for voice to draw intonation curve...
voicePractice.audioTooLoud: Volume too loud — move away from the mic
voicePractice.audioNoisy: Background noise detected — find a quieter place
voicePractice.listenBeforeSubmit: Listen back before sending to AI
voicePractice.karaokeTooltip: Automatically highlight the current word as you read, using your browser's speech recognition
voicePractice.karaokeErrorNotAllowed: The browser blocked microphone access for speech recognition — go to site settings to grant permission.
voicePractice.karaokeErrorAudioCapture: Couldn't get a microphone signal for recognition (the mic may be in exclusive use by recording) — try reloading the page.
voicePractice.karaokeErrorNetwork: Speech recognition connection error — if you're using Brave/Coc Coc/another Chromium browser, this feature only works on official Google Chrome (chrome.com) since it needs Google's own recognition server.
voicePractice.karaokeErrorGeneric: Speech recognition error ({{error}}) — toggle Karaoke off and on to retry.
voicePractice.karaokeIdleHint: Press Start Recording to have words auto-highlight as you speak
voicePractice.syncPlaybackTooltip: Play back the recording with words highlighted to match what was actually said (Whisper), more accurate than live karaoke during recording
voicePractice.noteSaveHint: Enter to save · Shift+Enter for a new line
voicePractice.pdfExportError: Error generating PDF. Please try again.
voicePractice.jitterTooltip: Fundamental frequency variation. < 1% = stable, 1-2% = acceptable, > 2% = shaky/uneven voice
voicePractice.shimmerTooltip: Amplitude variation. < 3% = good, 3-5% = normal, > 5% = weak or tired voice
voicePractice.hnrTooltip: Harmonics-to-Noise Ratio. ≥ 15dB = clear voice, 10-15dB = acceptable, < 10dB = noisy
voicePractice.spectralCentroidTooltip: Spectral centroid. Higher values = brighter, clearer voice. Ideal for MCs: ≥ 1500 Hz.
voicePractice.mfccStabilityTooltip: Sound structure stability (MFCC). ≥ 50/100 = consistent, stable pronunciation. Low = uneven pronunciation.
voicePractice.pitchContourTooltip: Voice pitch trend. Rising (↗) = pitch goes up, usually a question. Falling (↘) = pitch goes down, a statement. Flat (→) = steady.
voicePractice.fillerWordsTooltip: Unnecessary filler words (um, uh, like, so...). More filler words → less professional. MCs should aim for ≤ 2%.
voicePractice.werTooltip: Word Error Rate — percentage of words misrecognized. Professional MCs should achieve < 15%. Lower is more accurate.
voicePractice.cerTooltip: Character Error Rate — percentage of characters misrecognized. Reflects clarity of individual sounds. Good: < 10%.
voicePractice.overallScoreCardTooltip: Composite score weighted by the lesson's criteria (Pronunciation, Expressiveness, Rhythm...)
voicePractice.wpmCardTooltip: Words Per Minute — speaking rate. Professional MCs: 120-165 WPM.
voicePractice.guestTrialBanner: You're on a <1>free trial</1> — results won't be saved.
voicePractice.guestRegisterCta: Sign up free and get a day of any course →

voiceReport.coverageTooltip: Percentage of the script that was spoken. 100% = fully delivered.
voiceReport.paceTooltip: Words Per Minute — speaking rate. Professional MC range: 120-165 WPM.
voiceReport.criteriaPronunciationTooltip: How accurately each word and syllable was pronounced.
voiceReport.criteriaRhythmTooltip: Rhythm and intonation matching the context.
voiceReport.criteriaPacingTooltip: Whether speaking speed matches the target WPM.
voiceReport.criteriaEmotionTooltip: Level of emotional expressiveness in the voice.
voiceReport.criteriaAccuracyTooltip: How closely the spoken text matches the original script.
voiceReport.jitterTooltip: Fundamental frequency variation. < 1% = stable, 1-2% = acceptable, > 2% = shaky/unstable voice
voiceReport.shimmerTooltip: Amplitude variation. < 3% = good, 3-5% = normal, > 5% = weak or tired voice
voiceReport.hnrTooltip: Harmonics-to-noise ratio. ≥ 15dB = clear voice, 10-15dB = acceptable, < 10dB = noisy
voiceReport.spectralCentroidTooltip: Spectral center of mass. Higher = brighter, clearer voice. Ideal for MCs: ≥ 1500 Hz.
voiceReport.mfccStabilityTooltip: Vocal texture stability (MFCC). ≥ 50/100 = consistent, stable articulation.
voiceReport.pitchContourTooltip: Pitch trend direction. Rising (↗) = pitch goes up. Falling (↘) = pitch goes down. Flat (→) = steady.
voiceReport.fillerWordsTooltip: Unnecessary filler words (um, uh, like...). MCs should keep this under 2% of total words.

suggestions.inactiveLabel: Hasn't logged in for a while ({{days}} days)
suggestions.inactiveSubject: We miss you! — MCHub Voice Training
suggestions.inactiveContent:
Hi {{name}},

We noticed you haven't logged in to MCHub Voice Training in {{days}} days.

Come back and practice today — just 15 minutes a day can significantly improve your professional MC voice!

Visit: https://mchub.vn

MCHub Team
suggestions.activeLearnerContent:
Hi {{name}},

You've completed {{count}} practice sessions — that's fantastic!

Your average score is {{score}}/100. Keep up this momentum.

If you need further guidance on your MC development path, the MCHub team is always ready to help.

MCHub Team
suggestions.lowScoreSubject: Tips to improve your MC voice — MCHub
suggestions.lowScoreContent:
Hi {{name}},

We noticed you're putting in effort practicing. Your current average score: {{score}}/100.

Some tips to improve:
• Practice consistently every day, at least 10 minutes
• Focus on rhythm and intonation lessons
• Listen back to your recordings to self-assess

We hope you reach a high score soon!

MCHub Team
suggestions.highScoreContent:
Hi {{name}},

You're achieving an average score of {{score}}/100 — among the top learners on the platform!

MCHub appreciates your effort. Share your journey with the MC community to inspire others.

MCHub Team
suggestions.streakContent:
Hi {{name}},

You're on a {{count}}-day practice streak — impressive!

Your personal record is {{longest}} days. Keep going to beat your record!

MCHub Team
suggestions.upgradeLabel: Currently on FREE plan — upgrade suggestion

Hi {{name}},

You're currently using MCHub's FREE plan. To take full advantage of all lessons, advanced AI analysis, and unlimited practice sessions, explore our Premium plans.

See details at: https://mchub.vn/pricing

MCHub Team

dashboard-overview.title: Read overview KPIs
dashboard-overview.summary: Quickly grasp the 4 key metrics: revenue, transactions, users.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Dashboard (the default landing page).
steps.1: Top row of 4 KPI cards: Total Users · Successful Transactions · Total Transactions · Actual Revenue.
steps.2: The "Successful Transactions" card also shows the Pending and Failed counts below it.
steps.3: The "Actual Revenue" card only counts COMPLETED transactions — PENDING/FAILED are excluded.
steps.4: Data is live from MongoDB — press F5 to refresh.
steps.5: If all cards show 0, check that the backend is running (Admin → Server Logs).

dashboard-revenue-chart.title: Read the monthly revenue chart
dashboard-revenue-chart.summary: Analyze revenue trends via the area chart.

steps.0: The "Monthly Revenue" area chart sits in the middle of the Dashboard.
steps.1: X-axis = month (Jan–Dec) · Y-axis = revenue (in VND).
steps.2: Hover over a data point → tooltip shows the exact amount.
steps.3: Months with no COMPLETED transactions show 0 — this is normal.
steps.4: The chart only shows the current year. Previous-year data is not shown here.

dashboard-user-donut.title: Read the user distribution by role
dashboard-user-donut.summary: View the MC / Client ratio in the system.

steps.0: The "User Distribution" donut chart shows the MC (blue) / Client (green) ratio.
steps.1: ADMIN accounts are not counted in this chart.
steps.2: Hover over a slice → tooltip shows the count and corresponding percentage.
steps.3: If you only see one color, all current users share the same role.

dashboard-plan-bar.title: Read revenue by subscription plan
dashboard-plan-bar.summary: Compare the contribution of BASIC / FULL / ANNUAL plans.

steps.0: The bar chart at the bottom of the Dashboard shows revenue per plan.
steps.1: Click a bar to quickly filter over to the Transactions tab (if linked).
steps.2: The FREE plan contributes no revenue — its bar is always 0.
steps.3: Use this chart to decide which plan needs a promotion to boost conversion.

user-view.title: View and search the user list
user-view.summary: Find users by name/email, filter by role.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Users.
steps.1: Use the Search box (top corner) to search by name or email (case-insensitive).
steps.2: Use the Role filter chips (ALL / MC / CLIENT / ADMIN) to narrow the list.
steps.3: The table shows: Avatar · Name · Email · Role · Status (Active/Suspended) · Verified.
steps.4: Click a row to open the Side Panel with 3 tabs: Info · Stats · Notify.
steps.5: If the list is empty after filtering, try clearing the filter and searching again.

user-detail-info.title: View a user's detail info
user-detail-info.summary: Read the full profile: avatar, plan, status.

steps.0: Click a user in the table → the Side Panel opens on the right.
steps.1: The Info tab shows: Name, Email, Role, Current Plan, Created Date, Lock/Unlock status.
steps.2: Green badge = MC Verified · Yellow badge = not yet verified.
steps.3: Red badge = Suspended · Blue badge = Active.
steps.4: Current Plan indicates whether the user is on Free/Basic/Full/Annual.

user-detail-stats.title: View a user's practice statistics
user-detail-stats.summary: Check the user's activity level, score, and streak.

steps.0: Click a user → Side Panel → Stats tab.
steps.1: Metrics: Total Sessions · Average Score · Current Streak · Completed Lessons.
steps.2: Use this to identify inactive users (0 sessions in 30 days) → needs re-engagement.
steps.3: New users with no practice yet will show all 0s — this is normal.

user-add.title: Manually add a new user
user-add.summary: Create an account for an internal member or for testing.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Users → click "Add" (top-right corner).
steps.1: Fill in: Display Name, Email (must be unique), Password (≥6 characters).
steps.2: Choose Role: ADMIN / MC / CLIENT.
steps.3: Click "Create" — the user appears in the table immediately.
steps.4: A duplicate email will show an error — check for duplicates before creating.
steps.5: Do not grant ADMIN to anyone outside the team — it has full system access.
steps.6: Manually created users default to the FREE plan.

user-verify-mc.title: Verify / Unverify an MC
user-verify-mc.summary: Verify or revoke the professional MC badge.

steps.0: Find a user with the MC role in the table (filter Role = MC).
steps.1: Click the row → Side Panel → Info tab.
steps.2: Click "Verify MC" to verify → a blue badge appears on the profile.
steps.3: Click "Unverify" to revoke the badge if the MC violated the rules.
steps.4: The Verified badge is visible to other users viewing the MC profile.
steps.5: Only verify an MC after confirming their real information.

user-suspend.title: Suspend / Unlock an account
user-suspend.summary: Temporarily suspend login access for a violating user.

steps.0: Click a user → Side Panel → Info tab.
steps.1: Click "Suspend" to lock — the user loses login access immediately.
steps.2: Click "Unlock" to restore — the user can log in normally again.
steps.3: Suspending an account does not delete the user's data.
steps.4: If the user is online when suspended, their current token is invalidated immediately — they are logged out.
steps.5: Use this feature for rule violations, spam, or suspicious accounts.

user-password.title: Reset a user's password
user-password.summary: Admin sets a new password when a user forgets theirs or needs a reset.

steps.0: Click a user → Side Panel → Info tab.
steps.1: Click "Change Password" → enter a new password (≥6 characters).
steps.2: Click "Update" — the password changes immediately.
steps.3: The password cannot be viewed again after saving — use a temporary password and ask the user to change it.
steps.4: Notify the user of the temporary password via email or Zalo.
steps.5: You do not need to know the old password to perform this action.

user-notify-single.title: Send a personal email to a single user
user-notify-single.summary: Send an email directly to a specific account.

steps.0: Click a user → Side Panel → Notify tab.
steps.1: The system suggests smart content based on the user's status (inactive / high score / streak, etc.) — click a suggestion to pre-fill.
steps.2: Edit the Subject and Content as needed.
steps.3: Use {{name}} in the content — the system automatically replaces it with the user's name.
steps.4: Click "Send" — the email reaches the user's inbox within seconds.
steps.5: Do not send emails unrelated to the service — this may be reported as spam.
steps.6: Emails are sent via Brevo SMTP from themchubtraining@gmail.com.

user-delete.title: Delete a user account
user-delete.summary: Permanently delete a user from the system.

steps.0: Click a user → Side Panel → Info tab → find the "Delete Account" button (red, bottom of panel).
steps.1: The system requires confirmation — enter the user's email to confirm.
steps.2: Click "Confirm Delete".
steps.3: THIS CANNOT BE UNDONE. All the user's data, practice history, and transactions are deleted.
steps.4: Only delete when the user requests account deletion under GDPR or to confirm a fake account.
steps.5: Consider Suspend instead of Delete if you only want to temporarily block access.

lesson-view.title: View and search practice lessons
lesson-view.summary: Look up all MC scripts in the system.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Lessons.
steps.1: The table shows: Title · Category · Difficulty · Practice Count · Average Score.
steps.2: Use Search (by title) and the Category filter to narrow the list.
steps.3: Sort by name, creation date, or usage count.
steps.4: The lesson with the most usage is the most popular — keep its script quality high.

lesson-add.title: Add a new practice lesson
lesson-add.summary: Create a new MC script for learners to practice their voice.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Lessons → click "Add Practice Script".
steps.1: Enter a Title (concise, describes the event).
steps.2: Choose a Category: Wedding MC / Corporate Event / Talkshow MC / General.
steps.3: Enter the Script content in the textarea — write it like a real MC script (use [MC Name], [Guest Name]).
steps.4: Choose Difficulty: Easy (beginner) / Medium / Hard (professional).
steps.5: Add a short Description so learners know what skill this lesson practices.
steps.6: Upload a cover image (JPG/PNG, max 2MB) or leave it blank.
steps.7: Click Save — the lesson appears immediately in the Voice Library.
steps.8: A script that is too short (<50 words) is hard for the AI to analyze — aim for ≥150 words.
steps.9: Evaluation criteria are tied to the category — no additional configuration needed.

lesson-edit.title: Edit a practice lesson's content
lesson-edit.summary: Update the script, difficulty, and category of an existing lesson.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Lessons → find the lesson to edit.
steps.1: Click the pencil icon at the end of the row → the edit form opens.
steps.2: Edit the necessary fields.
steps.3: Click Save.
steps.4: Editing the script of a lesson currently used in an Academy Milestone affects the experience of learners currently on it — consider adding a new lesson instead of editing.

lesson-delete.title: Delete a practice lesson
lesson-delete.summary: Remove a lesson that is no longer relevant from the system.

steps.0: Find the lesson to delete → click the trash icon.
steps.1: The system warns if the lesson is currently assigned to an Academy Milestone.
steps.2: Confirm → the lesson is removed from the Voice Library and the admin list.
steps.3: Learners who already practiced this lesson keep their score history — they just can't practice it again.
steps.4: It's best to remove the lesson from the Academy Milestone before deleting it, to avoid display errors.

lesson-reading-guide.title: Manage Reading Guides
lesson-reading-guide.summary: View and add theory materials for learners.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Lessons → Reading Guides tab (if available).
steps.1: A Reading Guide is a theory document: speaking techniques, event coordination, MC style, etc.
steps.2: Add a guide: fill in Title, Content (Markdown supported), Category, Estimated reading time.
steps.3: Assign the guide to an Academy Milestone via the Academy module → Assign Content → READING_GUIDE.
steps.4: A Reading Guide does not require a mic — learners read and self-study the theory.

academy-overview.title: Understand the Academy structure
academy-overview.summary: Grasp the course logic: Course → Milestone → Content Item.

steps.0: The Academy has 3 levels: Course → Milestone/Stage → Content Item (a specific lesson).
steps.1: Example: Course "Wedding MC" → Milestone "Associate Level" → Item "Traditional Wedding Script".
steps.2: Learners must complete all items in a milestone to unlock the next milestone.
steps.3: 3 standard Levels: Associate (basic) · Professional (intermediate) · Elite (professional).

academy-course-create.title: Create a new Course
academy-course-create.summary: Initialize an overall course that contains milestones.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Academy → click "New Course".
steps.1: Fill in: Course Name, Description, Category (Wedding/Corporate/Talkshow, etc.).
steps.2: Upload a course cover image.
steps.3: Click Save — the created course has no milestones yet and is not visible to learners.
steps.4: Add milestones to the course (see the "Create Milestone" guide).
steps.5: A course only becomes visible to learners once it has at least 1 active milestone.

academy-milestone.title: Create a Milestone (learning stage)
academy-milestone.summary: Add a new stage to the learning path.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Academy → expand the course to add to → click "New Milestone Stage".
steps.1: Fill in: Stage Title, Level (Associate / Professional / Elite).
steps.2: Fill in a Description — learners see it when hovering over the milestone on the path.
steps.3: Set the Order number to arrange the sequence (1 = first).
steps.4: Click Save — the milestone appears as an expandable accordion for assigning content.
steps.5: Milestone order affects the learning path — set it correctly from the start.

academy-content.title: Assign content to a Milestone
academy-content.summary: Add a lesson or reading material to a stage.

steps.0: Expand the milestone to add content to → click "Assign Content".
steps.1: Choose the type: VOICE_PRACTICE (voice practice script) or READING_GUIDE (theory material).
steps.2: Search and select a lesson from the dropdown list.
steps.3: Set a Display Title within the milestone (can differ from the lesson's original name — optional).
steps.4: Choose the Estimated Duration: 5m / 10m / 15m / 30m / 45m / 1h.
steps.5: Click Save.
steps.6: Use the ▲▼ buttons on the right of each item to reorder within the milestone.
steps.7: The right panel previews the selected content before saving.

academy-content-reorder.title: Reorder content
academy-content-reorder.summary: Change the order of lessons within a milestone.

steps.0: Expand the milestone you want to reorder.
steps.1: Click ▲ to move an item up · Click ▼ to move it down.
steps.2: The order saves immediately — no need to press Save.
steps.3: Learners see this order when going through the learning path.

academy-content-remove.title: Remove content from a Milestone
academy-content-remove.summary: Delete an item from the milestone (does not delete the original lesson).

steps.0: Expand the milestone → find the item to remove → click the X icon or Delete.
steps.1: Confirm → the item is removed from the milestone.
steps.2: The original lesson remains in the Voice Library — only the link between the milestone and the lesson is removed.
steps.3: Learners currently on this milestone will see the removed item disappear — notify them beforehand if needed.

academy-quiz.title: Manage Quiz questions
academy-quiz.summary: Add/edit multiple-choice questions within a Course.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Academy → expand the course → Quiz Questions tab.
steps.1: Click "Add Question" → fill in the question, 4 answer options, the correct answer, and difficulty.
steps.2: Choose the type: MULTIPLE_CHOICE (1 answer) or TRUE_FALSE.
steps.3: Fill in the explanation so learners understand the correct answer.
steps.4: Click Save.
steps.5: The quiz appears at the end of the course — learners take it after completing all milestones.

competition-overview.title: Understand the competition system structure
competition-overview.summary: Grasp the Arena → Submission → Leaderboard logic.

steps.0: The competition system consists of: Arena (contest) → Submission (entry) → Leaderboard (ranking).
steps.1: Each Arena has one challenge script, a reset cycle (Daily/Weekly), and an active period.
steps.2: Learners record their entry and submit → AI scores it → it appears on the leaderboard.
steps.3: The leaderboard resets according to Interval: DAILY = resets at midnight every day · WEEKLY = resets at midnight on Monday.

competition-create.title: Create a new competition Arena
competition-create.summary: Launch a voice-training contest based on a specific script.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Competitions → click "New Contest Arena".
steps.1: Fill in: Arena Title, Short Description.
steps.2: Choose a Challenge Script: a script from the lesson list (voice practice).
steps.3: Choose the Contest Interval: DAILY (resets daily) or WEEKLY (resets weekly).
steps.4: Set the Start Date and End Date.
steps.5: Toggle Active on to activate — the Arena appears immediately for learners.
steps.7: It's recommended to run only 1 ACTIVE Arena at a time to concentrate participation.
steps.8: A script that's too long (>500 words) is hard for beginners — choose Medium/Easy for the Arena.

competition-edit.title: Edit / Deactivate an Arena
competition-edit.summary: Update timing, or deactivate a contest early.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Competitions → find the Arena to edit.
steps.1: Click Edit → adjust the end date, description, or toggle Active off.
steps.2: Click Save.
steps.3: Changing the Challenge script of an active Arena affects submissions still awaiting scoring — avoid changing it mid-contest.
steps.4: Turning off Active does not delete the leaderboard — results are still kept.

competition-leaderboard.title: View the leaderboard & submissions
competition-leaderboard.summary: Check who's leading and manage entries.

steps.0: Click into an Arena → Leaderboard tab.
steps.1: The leaderboard shows: Rank · Name · Avatar · Score · Submission Date.
steps.2: Click a submission to listen to the recording and see the detailed score per criterion.
steps.3: If a submission is found to be cheating (using voice AI) → click "Disqualify".
steps.4: Scores are graded by AI — if you suspect an error, review the detailed analysis before deciding.

plan-overview.title: Understand the subscription plan structure
plan-overview.summary: Learn the 4 plans and each plan's feature limits.

steps.0: 4 plans: FREE · BASIC · FULL · ANNUAL.
steps.1: FREE: basic practice, limited AI sessions per month.
steps.2: BASIC/FULL/ANNUAL: progressively higher AI session limits, unlocks advanced features.
steps.3: Go to Admin → Plans & Discounts to view and configure each plan.

plan-edit.title: Update subscription plan details
plan-edit.summary: Change price, description, and feature limits.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Plans & Discounts → Plans tab.
steps.1: Expand the plan to edit (FREE / BASIC / FULL / ANNUAL).
steps.2: Edit: Display Name, Price (VND), Duration (days), AI sessions/month limit.
steps.3: Update the Tagline and the list of Highlighted Features (one feature per line).
steps.4: Toggle Active to show/hide the plan from users.
steps.5: Click "Save changes".
steps.6: Price changes do NOT affect users already on that plan — only new subscribers.
steps.7: Don't disable Active on a plan with many active users — they won't be able to renew.

plan-discount.title: Set a direct discount on a plan
plan-discount.summary: Apply a % or fixed-amount discount directly on the pricing table.

steps.0: Expand the plan to discount → find the Discount section.
steps.1: Choose the type: Percent (%) or Fixed (fixed amount).
steps.2: Enter the value → the system automatically shows the discounted price for confirmation.
steps.3: Click "Save changes".
steps.4: This discount appears directly on the Pricing page — users see both the original and discounted price.
steps.5: To remove a discount: enter 0 in the Value field and save.

coupon-create.title: Create a discount Coupon
coupon-create.summary: Create a coupon for promotions or campaigns.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Plans & Discounts → Coupons tab → click "Create Coupon".
steps.1: Enter the Code (e.g. SUMMER30, WELCOME50) — uppercase, no spaces.
steps.2: Choose the Type: PERCENT (%) or FIXED (fixed VND amount).
steps.3: Enter the Value: e.g. 30 (= 30%) or 50000 (= 50,000 VND).
steps.4: Set Max Uses: the maximum number of redemptions (leave blank = unlimited).
steps.5: Set the Expiry Date.
steps.6: Choose Applicable Plans: which plans the coupon applies to (multiple selectable).
steps.8: The code automatically expires when the date passes or the usage limit is reached.
steps.9: The code is case-sensitive — a user entering "summer30" cannot redeem "SUMMER30".

steps.0: Go to Admin → Plans & Discounts → Coupons tab.
steps.1: The table shows: Code · Type · Value · Used/Max · Expiry Date · Status.
steps.2: Click the Active/Inactive toggle to enable/disable a code without deleting it.
steps.3: Click the trash icon → confirm → permanently delete the code.
steps.4: A code that has already been used can still be deleted — it only affects future redemptions.

transaction-view.title: View & search transactions
transaction-view.summary: Find payments by status, user, plan, or order code.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Transactions.
steps.1: Filter by Status chip: ALL / COMPLETED / PENDING / FAILED.
steps.2: Search in the search box: user name, email, plan name, payment memo, or order code.
steps.3: Sort: Newest / Oldest / Highest amount / Lowest amount.
steps.4: 3 overview cards at the top: Total Revenue · COMPLETED transactions · PENDING.

transaction-detail.title: View a single transaction's details
transaction-detail.summary: Check full information: user, plan, PayOS, timing.

steps.0: Click on a transaction row in the table.
steps.1: Details shown: Order Code, User, Subscription Plan, Amount, Status, Created Time, Completed Time.
steps.2: If a Coupon was used: shows the code applied and the discounted amount.
steps.3: The PayOS order code is used to reconcile with the payment gateway when verification is needed.

transaction-pending.title: Handle PENDING transactions
transaction-pending.summary: When a user reports they've paid but the transaction is still PENDING.

steps.0: Filter Status = PENDING to see the list awaiting processing.
steps.1: Check the PayOS order code on the PayOS admin portal (outside the MC Hub system).
steps.2: If PayOS has received the payment but the webhook hasn't fired → contact the dev to trigger the webhook manually.
steps.3: Do not manually change the status to COMPLETED in the DB unless the payment is confirmed.
steps.4: PENDING for over 24h usually indicates a webhook failure — escalate to dev.

transaction-refund.title: Handle refund requests
transaction-refund.summary: The process when a user requests a refund for a paid plan.

steps.0: Verify the corresponding COMPLETED transaction in Transactions.
steps.1: Go to the PayOS portal to process the refund from the payment gateway side.
steps.2: After the refund succeeds on PayOS, update the note in Admin (if a memo field exists).
steps.3: Go to Users → downgrade the user to the FREE plan or an appropriate plan.
steps.4: MC Hub does not auto-refund — it must be done directly on the PayOS portal.
steps.5: Log every refund in the internal tracking spreadsheet for reconciliation.

transaction-export.title: Export CSV report
transaction-export.summary: Download a CSV file for financial reporting and reconciliation.

steps.0: Filter the data you want to export (choose Status, search by plan or time range).
steps.1: Click the "Export CSV" button at the top right.
steps.2: The downloaded file contains: Order Code · User · Plan · Amount · Status · Created Date · Completed Date.
steps.3: The CSV exports the entire filtered result set — not paginated.
steps.4: Open with Google Sheets or Excel — use UTF-8 encoding to display Vietnamese text correctly.

notif-overview.title: Understand the notification creation and sending flow
notif-overview.summary: Grasp the 3 steps: Compose → Draft → Approve → Send.

steps.0: Notification flow: Compose → Save as DRAFT → Review → Actually send.
steps.1: DRAFT is not sent to anyone yet — it's only saved in the system for review.
steps.2: After sending: it cannot be recalled. Always preview first.
steps.3: The "History" tab stores all sent notifications for later reference.

notif-compose.title: Compose a new notification
notif-compose.summary: Create an email notification and save it as a Draft for approval.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Notifications → "+ Create New" tab.
steps.1: Choose the Notification Type: New Lesson / Promotion / Maintenance / Competition / New Feature / Social Post.
steps.2: Fill in the Internal Title (visible only to admins, to distinguish notifications).
steps.3: Fill in the Email Subject (the subject line the user sees in their inbox).
steps.4: Write the content — use {{name}} so the system auto-fills the recipient's name.
steps.5: Choose the recipients: ALL or filter by plan (FREE/BASIC/FULL/ANNUAL).
steps.6: Click "Preview email" to preview the email layout before sending.
steps.7: Click "Create notification" → saves as DRAFT status.
steps.8: Once sent, it cannot be recalled — always preview and review the Draft first.

notif-send.title: Approve and send notifications
notif-send.summary: Move a Draft to the actually-sent state.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Notifications → "Pending Approval" tab.
steps.1: View the list of pending DRAFT notifications.
steps.2: Click a notification to review its content and estimated recipient count.
steps.3: Click "Send" → confirm → the system sends emails asynchronously (one at a time).
steps.4: Send rate: ~1-2 emails/second via Brevo SMTP — 100 recipients ≈ 1-2 minutes.
steps.5: Do not send the same content twice within 24h — it may get flagged as spam.

notif-trigger.title: Use automatic notification triggers
notif-trigger.summary: Quickly create a Draft from a system-event template.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Notifications → "Auto Suggestions" tab.
steps.1: Choose the trigger matching the event you want to notify about.
steps.2: Fill in the trigger's specific variables (e.g. lessonTitle, discountPercent, code, date...).
steps.3: Click "Create draft" — the system generates the content and saves it as DRAFT.
steps.4: Go to the "Pending Approval" tab to review and send.
steps.5: Available triggers: New Lesson · Promotion · Maintenance · New Feature · Competition.

notif-history.title: View sent notification history
notif-history.summary: Look up the content and stats of past notifications.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Notifications → "History" tab.
steps.1: The table shows: Title · Type · Recipients · Sent Date · Status.
steps.2: Click a row to view the full content that was sent.
steps.3: Use this as reference when composing new notifications, to avoid repeating content.

notif-delete-draft.title: Delete a notification Draft
notif-delete-draft.summary: Cancel a draft notification that is no longer needed.

steps.0: Go to the "Pending Approval" tab → find the Draft to delete.
steps.1: Click the trash icon → confirm.
steps.2: The Draft is permanently deleted — if you need to send it again, create a new one from scratch.
steps.3: Sent notifications (status = SENT) cannot be deleted from the UI.

marketing-social-add.title: Add a Social Feed post
marketing-social-add.summary: Bring a Facebook/Instagram post into the homepage carousel.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Marketing → Social Feed tab.
steps.1: Click "Add post".
steps.2: Fill in the Post description (text shown below the image).
steps.3: Enter the Facebook post link (required) — format https://facebook.com/...
steps.4: Upload a thumbnail image (JPG/PNG) or enter an image URL directly.
steps.5: Set the Priority order — lower numbers display first in the carousel.
steps.6: Turn on the Active toggle to display it immediately on the homepage.
steps.7: Click Preview to see the carousel before saving.
steps.9: The homepage updates immediately — no backend refresh needed.

marketing-social-manage.title: Edit / Hide / Delete a Social Feed post
marketing-social-manage.summary: Update or remove a posted item.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Marketing → Social Feed tab.
steps.1: Find the post to act on in the list.
steps.2: Hide temporarily: turn off the Active toggle → post no longer shows on the homepage but stays saved.
steps.3: Edit: click Edit → adjust fields → Save.
steps.4: Delete: click the trash icon → confirm → the post is permanently deleted.

marketing-template-create.title: Create a campaign Email Template
marketing-template-create.summary: Design a reusable email template for multiple campaigns.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Marketing → Email Campaigns tab → Templates tab → click "Create template".
steps.1: Choose the editing mode: Design Builder (fill in fields visually) or Raw HTML (paste HTML).
steps.2: Design Builder: fill in Logo URL, Banner URL, Headline, Main content, CTA button (text + link).
steps.3: The right panel shows a real-time email preview as you type.
steps.4: Click "Test" → enter a test email → click "Send test" to check a real inbox.
steps.5: Click "Create template" to save.
steps.6: Use {{name}} in the HTML to personalize the recipient's name.
steps.7: Images in the HTML must be public URLs (https://...) — not localhost or local files.

marketing-template-edit.title: Edit / Delete an Email Template
marketing-template-edit.summary: Update template content or clean up old templates.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Marketing → Email Campaigns → Templates tab.
steps.1: Find the template to edit → click Edit.
steps.2: Modify the content → click "Update".
steps.3: Click the trash icon → confirm → deletes the template.
steps.4: Do not delete a template that is being used by a campaign that hasn't finished sending.

marketing-template-test.title: Send a template test email
marketing-template-test.summary: Verify a template before using it in a real campaign.

steps.0: Open the template to test (Edit or view details).
steps.1: Click the "Test" button → enter the email address to receive the test.
steps.2: Click "Send test" → check the inbox within a few seconds.
steps.3: Check: layout displays correctly, images load, CTA link works.
steps.4: If images don't display in the test email, the image URL may be blocked or not public.
steps.5: Send tests to several different email clients (Gmail, Outlook) to check compatibility.

marketing-campaign-send.title: Send a bulk email campaign
marketing-campaign-send.summary: Choose a template → choose recipients → send the campaign.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Marketing → Email Campaigns → Send Campaign tab.
steps.1: Choose a template from the dropdown — the right panel shows a template preview.
steps.2: Adjust the Subject (email title) if needed.
steps.3: Choose the Audience: All / By plan / By role / Premium / Custom emails.
steps.4: The system automatically loads the recipient list — you can tick/untick individual recipients.
steps.5: Click "Send campaign" → confirm the recipient count.
steps.6: Track progress in the History tab (SENDING → COMPLETED).
steps.7: Maximum 1 campaign/day for the same user group — to avoid spam.
steps.8: When sending, the system automatically deduplicates repeated emails — no need to worry about sending twice to the same person.

marketing-campaign-custom.title: Send a campaign to a custom email list
marketing-campaign-custom.summary: Manually enter specific emails to send a targeted campaign.

steps.0: Go to the Send Campaign tab → choose a template.
steps.1: Choose Audience: "Custom emails".
steps.2: Type each email into the field → press Enter to add it (shown as a chip).
steps.3: Click "Load list" to confirm the emails exist in the system and preview them.
steps.4: Tick/untick individual recipients in the confirmation list.
steps.5: Click "Send campaign".
steps.6: Emails that don't exist in the system will be skipped when sending.

marketing-campaign-history.title: View campaign history
marketing-campaign-history.summary: Track the results and status of sent campaigns.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Marketing → Email Campaigns → History tab.
steps.1: The table shows: Campaign Name · Template · Recipient Count · Status · Sent Date.
steps.2: Status: SENDING (in progress) · COMPLETED (done) · FAILED (error).
steps.3: Click a campaign → view the list of sent emails and the result of each one.
steps.4: Use this to audit sent content and avoid duplicate sends.

logs-overview.title: Understand the Server Logs interface
logs-overview.summary: Grasp the 3 panels: Live Logs · Watchlist · Bookmarks.

steps.0: The Server Logs page has 3 areas: Live Logs (center) · Watchlist (top right) · Bookmarks (bottom right).
steps.1: Live Logs: real-time log stream from the backend via WebSocket.
steps.2: Watchlist: configure automatic alerts (keyword + threshold).
steps.3: Bookmarks: list of logs marked for later investigation.

logs-monitor.title: Monitor logs in real time
logs-monitor.summary: View live JAVA/AI backend logs, filtered by level.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Server Logs → Live Logs panel.
steps.1: Top indicator: green = Connected · red = Offline (backend not running).
steps.2: Filter by Level: ALL / DEBUG / INFO / WARN / ERROR.
steps.3: Filter by Source: ALL / JAVA / AI (which backend is being displayed).
steps.4: Search text — results highlight in real time in the log stream.
steps.5: Click Pause to stop scrolling (new logs still buffer) · Click again to resume.
steps.6: Click Group to collapse logs with the same error into one group.
steps.7: Click a log line → Detail Panel on the right: Info · API · User · Note tabs.

logs-read-error.title: Read and understand ERROR logs
logs-read-error.summary: Analyze ERROR logs to report accurate information to developers.

steps.0: Filter Level = ERROR to see only critical errors.
steps.1: Click an error line → Detail Panel → Info tab: time, service, message.
steps.2: API tab: the failing endpoint, HTTP method, response code.
steps.3: User tab: related userId (if any).
steps.4: Bookmark the error log → Note tab → record when it was found and the context.
steps.5: When reporting to developers, provide: Time · Error message · Endpoint · UserId (if any).

logs-watchlist.title: Set up automatic Watchlist alerts
logs-watchlist.summary: Get browser alerts when a log matches a keyword or too many errors occur.

steps.0: Go to Admin → Server Logs → Watchlist panel.
steps.1: Set the Error Threshold: number of ERRORs/minute to trigger a browser notification.
steps.2: Add Keywords to watch: type the keyword → press Enter → appears as a chip.
steps.3: Logs matching a keyword auto-highlight in red in Live Logs.
steps.4: When the threshold is exceeded: a browser notification pops up (notifications must be allowed).
steps.5: Suggested keywords to watch: "OutOfMemoryError", "SocketTimeout", "NullPointer", "401".

logs-bookmark.title: Bookmark logs and add investigation notes
logs-bookmark.summary: Mark important logs and add notes to revisit them later.

steps.0: Hover over the log line to mark → click the bookmark icon.
steps.1: The log appears in the Bookmarks panel (right sidebar).
steps.2: Detail Panel → Note tab → type investigation notes (no length limit).
steps.3: Click a bookmark in the list → returns to Logs with that log highlighted.
steps.4: Click the X icon on a bookmark to remove it when no longer needed.
steps.5: Bookmarks are stored in browser localStorage — clearing the cache removes them.

logs-backend-down.title: Handle backend Offline status
logs-backend-down.summary: Check and escalate when the indicator turns red.

steps.0: If the indicator shows red "Offline", the Java or AI backend is not running.
steps.1: Check health by visiting: http://localhost:5000/actuator/health (if you still have server access).
steps.2: If it cannot be restarted automatically, contact a developer or DevOps immediately.
steps.3: Record the time the outage was detected in internal logs.
steps.4: When the backend is offline: all AI, practice, and login features stop working.
steps.5: Wait 2-3 minutes before reporting — the backend may be restarting automatically.
